incite-greed.canonicalEvidence: Choose any number of creatures within range that can see you.
incite-greed.structuredState: Targeting Type: single; Valid Targets: creatures_visual.
incite-greed.jsonState: Runtime targeting is single, maxTargets 1, creatures.
incite-greed.issue: Incite Greed can target any number of visible creatures, but structured and runtime targeting are still single-target.
incite-greed.recommendedTemplateChange: Add unlimited selected-target count support and visible-target requirement fields.
incite-greed.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime targeting support for any-number selected targets that can see the caster/gem.

incite-greed.canonicalEvidence: Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you ... At the end of each of its turns, an affected target can make a Wisdom saving throw. If it succeeds, this effect ends for that target.
incite-greed.structuredState: Save Stat: Wisdom; Save Outcome: negates.
incite-greed.jsonState: Runtime Charmed effect uses a Wisdom save gate and keeps the real turn_end Wisdom repeat save.
incite-greed.issue: The initial and repeat Wisdom saves are represented; non-save break and behavior restrictions are tracked separately.
incite-greed.recommendedTemplateChange: No save-field change required for this row.
incite-greed.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by preserving the turn_end repeat save and removing only the noncanonical escapeCheck.

incite-greed.canonicalEvidence: At the end of each of its turns, an affected target can make a Wisdom saving throw. If it succeeds, this effect ends for that target.
incite-greed.structuredState: No explicit repeat-save field, but Save Stat/Outcome preserve the save family.
incite-greed.jsonState: Runtime statusCondition.repeatSave records turn_end Wisdom successEnds true.
incite-greed.issue: The end-turn repeat save is represented in runtime JSON.
incite-greed.recommendedTemplateChange: Optional future structured repeat-save field would make this visible in markdown, but no runtime correction is needed.

incite-greed.canonicalEvidence: You present the gem used as the material component and choose any number of creatures within range that can see you.
incite-greed.structuredState: Line of Sight: true; Valid Targets: creatures_visual.
incite-greed.jsonState: Runtime lineOfSight is true, but does not encode targets seeing the caster/gem presentation.
incite-greed.issue: Incite Greed depends on target visibility of the caster and presented gem, not just caster line of sight.
incite-greed.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime condition requiring targets to see the caster/presented gem.

incite-greed.canonicalEvidence: While charmed in this way, a creature can do nothing but use its movement to approach you in a safe manner.
incite-greed.structuredState: Conditions Applied: Charmed; no compelled-behavior fields.
incite-greed.jsonState: Runtime JSON applies Charmed but has no forced behavior or safe-approach movement rule.
incite-greed.issue: Incite Greed's charm imposes a specific compelled behavior that is not represented by the generic Charmed condition.
incite-greed.recommendedTemplateChange: Add compelledBehavior fields for allowed actions and safe approach target.
incite-greed.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime compelledBehavior data: only movement toward caster by safe route.

incite-greed.canonicalEvidence: While an affected creature is within 5 feet of you, it cannot move, but simply stares greedily at the gem you present.
incite-greed.jsonState: Runtime JSON has Charmed but no within-5-feet cannot-move rider.
incite-greed.issue: Incite Greed applies a proximity-gated movement lock that is not represented.
incite-greed.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime movementRestriction data for cannot move while within 5 feet of caster.

incite-greed.canonicalEvidence: The creature is charmed until the spell ends, or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it. If the end-turn save succeeds, this effect ends for that target.
incite-greed.structuredState: Conditional Ending Triggers records caster_or_companion_harms_target and repeat_save_success with effect scope.
incite-greed.jsonState: Runtime JSON records conditionalEndings for caster_or_companion_harms_target and repeat_save_success on the Charmed status effect.
incite-greed.issue: Closed: Incite Greed's harmful-action break condition and repeat-save effect ending are now represented.
incite-greed.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by adding caster_or_companion_harms_target to the conditional-ending trigger vocabulary and using it with repeat_save_success on Incite Greed.
incite-greed.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by runtime conditionalEndings on the status effect for both harmful action by the caster/companions and the successful repeat save.
incite-greed.closedReason: Incite Greed now records its per-target Charmed effect endings in structured markdown and runtime JSON. A new caster_or_companion_harms_target trigger preserves the broader canonical harmful-action wording instead of flattening it into damage-only.

intellect-fortress.canonicalEvidence: For the duration, you or one willing creature you can see within range has resistance to psychic damage, as well as advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.
intellect-fortress.structuredState: Effect Type: BUFF; no saving-throw advantage fields.
intellect-fortress.jsonState: Runtime JSON has a prose UTILITY effect with no typed save modifier.
intellect-fortress.issue: Intellect Fortress grants Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throw advantage, but this is prose-only.
intellect-fortress.recommendedTemplateChange: Add savingThrowAdvantage fields for ability lists.
intellect-fortress.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime saveModifier data for Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma advantage.

intellect-fortress.canonicalEvidence: You or one willing creature you can see within range.
intellect-fortress.issue: The caster sight requirement is represented; willingness and self-or-creature targeting are tracked separately.
intellect-fortress.recommendedTemplateChange: No line-of-sight change required for this row.

intellect-fortress.canonicalEvidence: You or one willing creature you can see within range has resistance to psychic damage.
intellect-fortress.structuredState: Effect Type: BUFF; no resistance fields.
intellect-fortress.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no DEFENSIVE resistance effect.
intellect-fortress.issue: Intellect Fortress's psychic resistance is not represented.
intellect-fortress.recommendedTemplateChange: Use DEFENSIVE resistance fields for Psychic damage.
intellect-fortress.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime DEFENSIVE resistance for Psychic damage.
intellect-fortress.reviewerNote: Closed by adding DEFENSIVE resistance in structured markdown and runtime JSON with Damage Type Psychic. Save advantage and targeting/scaling gaps remain tracked in their separate buckets.
